page: Microsoft Azure GS1 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 40 MB of L3 cache, 32 KB of L1D cache, and 0.25 MB of L2 cache, with support for AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets. The server is equipped with 28.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.0 GB per core, alongside 57 GB of local storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the GS1 is constrained in compute-heavy tasks. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks fall into the bottom 10%, and Geekbench multi-core scores are similarly positioned. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor performance, with read-only large block bandwidth at 20.49 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 13.0 GB/sec. However, single-threaded gzip compression achieves an average rating at 23.85 GB/sec. Redis SET operations perform at 3.03 million operations per second, which is classified as weak.

From a resource density perspective, the GS1 offers a high memory allocation relative to its low vCPU count, making it a specialized option for workloads requiring significant RAM without demanding high-performance compute. The primary tradeoff is the older Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 architecture, which limits processing throughput and instruction execution speeds compared to newer hardware generations. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice specifically for legacy memory-bound tasks rather than compute-intensive applications.

Given these specifications and benchmark results, the Microsoft Azure GS1 is best suited for lightweight database hosting, caching layers, basic memory-bound data processing, and legacy enterprise applications that require a high memory footprint but minimal CPU processing power.
description: Microsoft Azure GS1 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 28.0 GB of RAM (14.0 GB per core) and 57 GB of local storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark data shows constrained compute capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores in the bottom 10%, though single-threaded gzip compression performs at an average level of 23.85 GB/sec. The server presents a clear tradeoff, offering high memory density relative to its low processing power. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for lightweight databases, caching layers, and legacy memory-bound applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-core density and local storage for low-compute, memory-bound legacy workloads.
